description: Lock the 2026-06-24 rescope of merchant_risk_pooling to medieval sea-loan, commenda, and early insurance-style maritime risk-sharing contracts.

checks.reason: The node is now anchored to twelfth- and thirteenth-century medieval Italian maritime risk-sharing practice, not a broad 1250 finance placeholder.
The scoped technology is risk-sharing finance, not generic ship-hire charterparty contracting.
Bills-of-exchange clearing settles debts but is not source-backed as a direct prerequisite for sea-loan or commenda risk sharing.
Long-distance maritime trade routes created the repeated risks these contracts allocated.
Notarial deeds are the documented legal form for the source-attested medieval insurance-style contracts.
Contract-enforcement institutions supported commitment to sea-loan and commenda risk-sharing contracts.
